skill.name: temporal-python-pro
skill.description: Master Temporal workflow orchestration with Python SDK. Implements durable workflows, saga patterns, and distributed transactions. Covers async/await, testing strategies, and production deployment. Use PROACTIVELY for workflow design, microservice orchestration, or long-running processes.
skill.summary: Build durable Temporal workflows, activities, retries, tests, and production worker patterns with the Python SDK.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported 30 potential issues, but review found Markdown guidance rather than executable code. Backtick, weak-crypto, and reconnaissance alerts are false positives from inline identifiers, YAML prose, and documentation text.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Inline Identifiers
low_findings.description: The external command alerts are Markdown backticks around Temporal Python decorators, methods, and filenames. No shell, Ruby, or command execution code is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain only Markdown inline code for Temporal APIs and Python examples. There is no executable script block or command invocation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Crypto Keywords in Prose
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm alerts match ordinary description and section text. No cryptographic function, hash call, or security-sensitive implementation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations are YAML description and prose about workflow design. I found no code that imports or calls weak cryptographic algorithms.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance Keyword in Documentation
low_findings.description: The network reconnaissance alert points to a sentence about read-only workflow state access. It does not invoke scanning tools or enumerate networks.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is descriptive documentation for workflow queries. There is no network command, endpoint probing, or reconnaissance workflow.

content.value_statement: Long-running Python services need durable orchestration, retries, and clear failure handling. This skill guides Temporal workflow design, activity patterns, testing, and production deployment.
actual_capabilities: Guides worker configuration, task queues, workflow registration, and activity registration.
Explains deterministic workflow patterns using Temporal Python SDK concepts.
Covers retry policies, timeout choices, compensation logic, and ApplicationError usage.
Describes signal, query, state management, and workflow versioning patterns.
Outlines testing with time skipping, activity tests, integration tests, and replay checks.
Provides production guidance for scaling, graceful shutdown, metrics, tracing, and rollout safety.
limitations: It provides guidance only and does not include executable project files.
It references an implementation playbook that is not included in the package.
It does not replace official Temporal documentation for version-specific API details.
It cannot inspect a live Temporal cluster without user-provided data.

use_cases.title: Design a Durable Order Workflow
use_cases.description: Map order steps, retries, compensation, signals, and queries before implementation.

use_cases.title: Review Worker Production Readiness
use_cases.description: Check worker startup, task queues, shutdown behavior, concurrency, metrics, and deployment risks.

use_cases.title: Plan Workflow Test Coverage
use_cases.description: Choose unit, integration, time-skipping, and replay tests for long-running Temporal workflows.

prompt_templates.title: Explain the Workflow Shape
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning a first Temporal workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain how to model this process as a Temporal Python workflow. Include workflows, activities, retries, timeouts, and required signals.

prompt_templates.title: Review Activity Boundaries
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate user separating workflow logic from external calls
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my proposed Temporal Python workflow design. Identify which steps must be activities and which logic can remain deterministic.

prompt_templates.title: Design Failure Handling
prompt_templates.scenario: Experienced user implementing compensation and retries
prompt_templates.prompt: Design retry, timeout, and compensation behavior for this distributed transaction. Explain transient failures, permanent failures, and idempotency needs.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Production Readiness
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user preparing a deployment review
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this Temporal Python worker and workflow plan for production readiness. Cover deployment, observability, replay safety, scaling, and rollout risks.

output_examples.input: Design a payment and inventory workflow for an order service.
output: A workflow boundary that owns order state and orchestration.
Activities for payment authorization, inventory reservation, notification, and compensation.
Retry and timeout guidance for each external dependency.
Signals for cancellation and queries for order progress.

output_examples.input: Review why my Temporal workflow is not deterministic.
output: A list of likely determinism violations.
Safer Temporal SDK alternatives for time, randomness, and external calls.
Replay testing steps to confirm compatibility.

output_examples.input: Create a testing strategy for a month-long approval workflow.
output: Mock activity cases for approval, rejection, timeout, and cancellation.
Integration and replay checks for release validation.

best_practices: Keep workflow code deterministic and move external calls into activities.
Set explicit timeouts, retry policies, and idempotency rules for every activity.
Use replay tests before changing workflow logic that may affect existing histories.
anti_patterns: Calling external APIs directly from workflow code.
Running blocking libraries inside async activities without an executor strategy.
Deploying worker changes without task queue, versioning, and rollback planning.

faq.question: Does this skill generate complete Temporal applications?
faq.answer: No. It provides architecture, implementation guidance, review checklists, and testing plans.

faq.question: Can it help with saga patterns?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers compensation logic, retries, permanent failures, and distributed transaction boundaries.

faq.question: Does it support async Python activities?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains async activities, sync threaded activities, and sync multiprocess activities.

faq.question: Can it review determinism risks?
faq.answer: Yes. It highlights unsafe time, randomness, threading, global state, and direct external calls.

faq.question: Does it include deployment guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers worker scaling, graceful shutdown, observability, task queues, and rollout patterns.

faq.question: Is this an official Temporal skill?
faq.answer: No. It is a community skill and should be checked against current Temporal documentation.
